name: Yamato Takeru
description:
Yamato Takeru, a legendary prince of the Yamato dynasty, is a central figure in Japanese mythology and folklore. Born Prince Ousu, he was the son of Emperor Keikō and renowned for his bravery and martial prowess. His story is primarily recounted in the Kojiki and Nihon Shoki, ancient chronicles that blend historical narratives with mythological elements. Yamato Takeru's early life was marked by a pivotal act of violence when he assassinated his elder brother, Prince Ōtarashi, on their father's orders, demonstrating his ruthless efficiency. He was then tasked by his father with subduing rebellious Kumaso chieftains in the south. Disguised as a woman, he infiltrated their celebratory feast and assassinated them both, earning the name 'Yamato Takeru,' meaning 'The Brave of Yamato.'

His exploits continued as he ventured east to pacify the eastern provinces, battling monstrous creatures and rebellious tribes. He faced treacherous landscapes and formidable opponents, always displaying unwavering courage and strategic brilliance. During his eastern campaign, he fell in love with Princess Oto Tachibana, who tragically sacrificed herself to appease the sea god and ensure his safe passage across the sea to Kazusa. This act of selfless love deeply affected Yamato Takeru.

Yamato Takeru's journey was fraught with peril, and he often received divine assistance, notably from his aunt, Princess Yamato-hime, the high priestess of Ise Grand Shrine, who provided him with the sword Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi. Despite his victories, he was ultimately cursed and died on his return journey, succumbing to illness and exhaustion. As he lay dying, he lamented his fate and transformed into a white bird, flying away to his final resting place. Yamato Takeru is revered as a symbol of courage, loyalty, and the embodiment of the Yamato spirit. His legends have inspired countless works of art, literature, and drama throughout Japanese history, solidifying his place as a national hero and a cornerstone of Japanese cultural identity.
subtitle: The Brave Prince of Yamato
achievements: Defeating various monsters and rebellious tribes

enemies.name: Various rebellious tribes of the East

keyMyths.title: Subjugation of the Kumaso
keyMyths.description: Yamato Takeru, disguised as a woman, infiltrated a celebratory feast of the Kumaso chieftains and assassinated them, proving his cunning and bravery. This act earned him the name 'Yamato Takeru,' meaning 'The Brave of Yamato.'

keyMyths.title: The Sacrifice of Oto Tachibana
keyMyths.description: During his journey to the east, Princess Oto Tachibana sacrificed herself to appease the sea god and ensure Yamato Takeru's safe passage across the sea to Kazusa. Her selfless act highlights the themes of love and sacrifice in the myth.

keyMyths.title: The Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi
keyMyths.description: Princess Yamato-hime, the high priestess of Ise Grand Shrine, gifted Yamato Takeru the legendary sword Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi, which aided him in his battles. This sword is one of the Three Sacred Treasures of Japan.

sources.author: Anonymous
